{"key_event":"Total war and democratic franchise expansion","reasoning":"The war state acquired exceptional powers—conscription, censorship, industrial direction, rationing, and vast taxation—pushing already high British state capacity close to the top of the scale despite limits in Ireland. Civil society also strengthened through labour organization, women’s mobilization, the weakened Lords, and the 1918 franchise expansion, but wartime controls over speech, unions, and industry kept the net gain modest.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":9.7,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":9.9}
